scenarioName: Create Booking - Client Already Has Non-Terminal Booking (409)
objective: Validate that a client cannot create a new booking if they already have a non-terminal booking (CONFIRMED or ACTIVE)

seedData.description: Client user with existing non-terminal booking
Venue for existing booking and target venue for new booking attempt
Existing CONFIRMED booking owned by the client (non-terminal)
Available roster slots for new booking attempt

payloadRules.venueId: Reference to target venue (different from existing booking)
payloadRules.startAt: Future ISO-8601 timestamp (different time from existing booking)

steps.layman: Client attempts to create a second booking while having an active CONFIRMED booking

expectedBehavior: Check for existing non-terminal bookings: SELECT id FROM bookings WHERE client_id={{CLIENT_ID}} AND status IN ('CONFIRMED', 'ACTIVE') LIMIT 1
steps.layman: System detects the client already has a non-terminal booking

steps.expectedErrorMessage: Client already has a non-terminal booking (CONFIRMED or ACTIVE)
steps.layman: System rejects the request with a conflict error
Verify only the original booking exists (no new booking created)

steps.expectedBehavior: Target roster slots remain AVAILABLE (not reserved)
steps.layman: Verify no roster slots were reserved for the rejected booking

businessRules: One non-terminal booking per client rule enforced
Non-terminal statuses are CONFIRMED and ACTIVE
CANCELLED and COMPLETED bookings do not block new bookings

notes: Tests the one non-terminal booking per client invariant
DB unique index provides ultimate enforcement
Can also test with ACTIVE status variant
